LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS. THE MOTION IS TRANSFERRED TO DEPARTMENT 501, TO BE HEARD AT 9:30 A.M., ON JANUARY 3, 2020. THAT DEPARTMENT HANDLES ALL MATTERS INVOLVING INJURY TO REAL PROPERTY PER SF LOCAL RULE 8.10A1. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR., CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/RBU)
MINI MINUTES FOR JAN-03-2020 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 501

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. MOTION IS DENIED AS TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INVERSE CONDEMNATION. ISSUES OF FACT, NOT SUSCEPTIBLE TO DETERMINATION AT A PLEADING STAGE, EXIST AS TO THE "INHERENT RISK, "SUBSTANTIAL CAUSE," AND "DELIBERATE DESIGN, CONTRACTION OR MAINTENANCE" OF THE TREE. CITY OF OROVILLE V. SUP. CT. (2019) 7 CAL.5TH 1091. MOTION IS OTHERWISE GRANTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO ALL OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION TO ALLEGE IN GOOD FAITH STATUTORY BASIS FOR LIABILITY AGAINST A PUBLIC ENTITY. ORDER IS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS.) JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. (D501)
MINUTES FOR FEB-03-2020 3:00 PM

MASTER EXPEDITED JURY CALENDAR SET FOR NOV-04-2019 - PLAINTIFF PAUL O'GRADY APPEARING AT TRIAL CALL WITHOUT HIS COUNSEL OF RECORD, JOHN LEE. HUNTER SIMS, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT. PLAINTIFF STATED TO THE COURT THAT HIS ATTORNEY JOHN LEE WILL NO LONGER REPRESENT HIM AND ORALLY REQUESTED A CONTINUANCE OF THE TRIAL DATE. DEFENDANT DID NOT OPPOSE CONTINUING THE TRIAL DATE. THE COURT GRANTED THE ORAL MOTION TO CONTINUE THE TRIAL DATE TO FEB-03-2020 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 206 ON CONDITION THAT PLAINTIFF PROCEED TO THE CLERK'S OFFICE ROOM 103 TO PAY THE $60 CONTINUANCE FEE AND RETURN WITH THE RECEIPT. THE COURT URGED PLAINTIFF TO SEEK NEW COUNSEL. JUDGE: GARRETT L. WONG; MATTER NOT REPORTED (206/GLW) [PLAINTIFF RETURNED TO DEPT 206 WITH $60 RECEIPT, TRIAL DATE OF FEB-03-2020 AT 9:30AM IN DEPT. 206 CONFIRMED.]

MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
Off Calendar Jan-03-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
() for Plaintiff/Moving party .
Frederick P. Sheinfield, esq. (415-554-3872) Deputy City Attorney for Defendant/Moving party.
No other appearances.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is DENIED as to the cause of action for inverse condemnation. Issues of fact, not susceptible to determination at a pleading stage, exist as to the "inherent risk, "substantial cause," and "deliberate design, contraction or maintenance" of the tree. City of Oroville v. Sup. Ct. (2019) 7 Cal.5th 1091. Motion is otherwise granted with leave to amend as to all other causes of action to allege in good faith statutory basis for liability against a public entity.

Order is signed in open court. (See Scanned Order for Details.)

Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. (D501)
